mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR, PDGFR and other kinases to block pro‑proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Clinically used in solid tumors; widely tested off‑label in FLT3‑ITD AML including MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, PDGFR and related receptor tyrosine kinases
Inhibits SRC, ABL and related kinases including LYN and KIT, disrupting oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML and Ph+ ALL

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, KIT and PDGFR, blocking oncogenic kinase signaling

category: BCR‑ABL / KIT / PDGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, KIT and PDGFR to reduce proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Particularly effective in KIT‑mutant core‑binding‑factor AML and PDGFR‑rearranged AML; less potent in FLT3‑driven cells

mechanism: Potently inhibits BCR‑ABL (including T315I) as well as FLT3 and KIT
additional_notes: Highly active in FLT3‑ITD AML lines such as MOLM‑13 and MV4‑11

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR and other kinases, suppressing proliferation signals
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutant AML

mechanism: Highly selective inhibition of FLT3, particularly FLT3‑ITD, leading to apoptosis of dependent AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; approved/late‑stage in FLT3‑ITD AML; strong activity in MOLM‑13 and MV4‑11

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL tyrosine kinases, blocking survival signaling in FLT3‑mutant AML
Type I inhibitor of FLT3 active against ITD and many TKD mutations, suppressing FLT3‑driven signaling
Binds and inhibits anti‑apoptotic protein BCL‑2 to promote apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; approved in AML in combination regimens; effective in BCL‑2–dependent lines including MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, reducing JAK/STAT pathway signaling involved in survival and proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis and polycythemia vera; can sensitize AML cells to other agents

mechanism: Selectively inhibits the PI3Kδ isoform, dampening PI3K/AKT survival signaling
additional_notes: Approved for B‑cell malignancies; PI3Kδ contributes to survival in some AML lines

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) and related TEC family kinases, disrupting B‑cell receptor–related signaling that can be co‑opted in AML
additional_notes: Approved for CLL and mantle cell lymphoma; BTK expressed in some AML, especially monocytic phenotypes

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, leading to increased histone acetylation, altered gene expression and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved for cutaneous T‑cell lymphoma (CTCL)

mechanism: Broadly inhibits class I/II HDACs, inducing hyperacetylation of histones and non‑histone proteins and promoting apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; very potent in AML cell lines

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and RNA and inhibits DNMTs, causing DNA hypomethylation and re‑expression of silenced genes
additional_notes: Approved for MDS and AML; requires longer exposure for full hypomethylating effects (often ≥96–120 h)

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and traps DNMTs, leading to DNA hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Approved for MDS and AML; activity often enhanced with prolonged exposure

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing accumulation of ubiquitinated proteins and induction of apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma; AML IC50 typically 5–50 nM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the 20S proteasome chymotrypsin‑like activity, leading to proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Second‑generation proteasome inhibitor; AML IC50 usually 5–50 nM in sensitive lines
